_comment: Curated SQL injection payloads. ARBITER's backend is DynamoDB (NoSQL) so a real SQL backend won't be hit, but these are still useful negative tests against any string-template logic (e.g. the partial /findings filters where severity/status/domain land in DDB FilterExpression strings). PASS = no 500, no stack trace, no payload reflection. Payloads sourced from PortSwigger SQL injection cheat sheet and OWASP Testing Guide.

payloads.rationale: Classic tautology — harmless against DDB but must not 500. expected_blocked=false because DDB will treat it as a literal string value.
Statement terminator + DROP — exercises any DDB FilterExpression that builds strings via f-string concatenation.
UNION-based exfil pattern. Harmless against DDB; verifies the API doesn't error on unbalanced quotes.
SQL comment to bypass password check — tests login-flow-style string handling even though /chat doesn't use SQL.
Time-based SQLi probe (T-SQL). API should reject as invalid input OR treat as literal string — never hang for 5s.
Boolean-based — common entry-level probe. Verifies the API handles unbalanced quotes without crashing.
Stacked-query attempt. DDB doesn't have INSERT, but verifies API doesn't crash on multi-statement payloads.
Hex-encoded version of ' OR 1=1-- — defeats keyword-based input filters.
Double-quote variant — common in JSON-body contexts where single quotes are unaffected.
